name: Starter design system
audience: Non-technical designers working with an AI coding agent
howToPreview: Ask the agent: Show me the design system gallery. It should run npm run gallery.

colors.use: Main actions, focus rings, informative emphasis
Page backgrounds, panels, dividers, quiet grouped areas

foundations.shape: Rounded corners are soft and practical. Cards and controls use rounded-lg by default.
foundations.density: Comfortable spacing for review-friendly mockups rather than dense production screens.

components.name: Avatar
components.useWhen: Showing a person, owner, reviewer, teammate, or collaborator.
components.designerPrompt: Use avatars to show who owns or reviews this item. Use initials if no photo is available.

components.name: Badge
components.useWhen: Showing compact status, category, priority, or confidence.
components.designerPrompt: Add a badge for the item's status, but keep the text short.

components.name: Button
components.designerPrompt: Use one primary button for the main action. Use secondary buttons for alternatives.

components.name: Card
components.useWhen: Grouping a focused piece of content or a small workflow.
components.designerPrompt: Use a card for one coherent object, not for every row on the page.

components.name: Input
components.useWhen: Capturing short text, names, emails, labels, and search-like input.
components.designerPrompt: Keep labels visible. Use placeholders as examples, not instructions.

components.name: Progress
components.useWhen: Showing completion percentage for setup, onboarding, upload, or review readiness.
components.designerPrompt: Use progress when the amount complete is meaningful. Add a short label beside it.

components.name: Switch
components.useWhen: Turning a setting on or off.
components.designerPrompt: Use switches for immediate settings, not one-time actions.

components.name: Tabs
components.useWhen: Switching between a few sibling views in the same area.
components.designerPrompt: Use tabs only when each view is equally important and closely related.

components.name: IconButton
components.useWhen: Compact secondary actions such as settings, copy, more, or close.
components.designerPrompt: Use icon buttons sparingly and give them accessible labels.

starterRequestsForAgents: Show me the design system gallery.
Create a small onboarding card using Button, Card, Badge, Input, Progress, and Switch.
Take screenshots of the design system gallery.
Make this mockup feel calmer while still using the client design system.
